channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Live artillery exercise to shut down interstate near Camp Pendleton
item.description: Governor Gavin Newsom has criticized the federal government for allowing a live-fire military demonstration at Camp Pendleton to take place near the heavily trafficked I-5 freeway, prompting a temporary closure of the highway and causing potential disruption to the local economy.
item.dc:creator: Amber Coakley, NewsNation

item.title: Legally blind Ohio artist says she can't drive, but she can tattoo
item.description: Nicole Morrone was born legally blind, but she still creates detailed, colorful artwork -- on her clients' skin.
item.dc:creator: Abigail Cloutier, Fox 5

item.title: Taiwan's main opposition party elects new leader in a race clouded by claims of China meddling
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — Taiwan ’s main opposition Nationalist Party chose a former lawmaker as its new chairperson on Saturday in a competitive election clouded by allegations of China’s meddling.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Shutdown impact: What it means for workers, federal programs and the economy
item.description: The federal government shutdown is approaching the second longest on record and there's no end in sight.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Freking, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Prince Andrew's antics and scandals have tried royal patience, triggering headlines and suspicions
item.description: Britain’s Prince Andrew was forced to relinquish use of his remaining royal titles after the latest revelations about his relationship with the convicted pedophile Jeffrey Epstein proved one scandal too many for his brother, King Charles III.
item.dc:creator: DANICA KIRKA, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Alaska storm damage so bad many evacuees won’t go home for at least 18 months, governor says
item.description: ANCHORAGE, Alaska (AP) — Damage to remote Alaska villages hammered by flooding last weekend is so extreme that many of the more than 2,000 people displaced won’t be able to return to their homes for at least 18 months, Gov.
item.dc:creator: MARK THIESSEN and GENE JOHNSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: RECALL: Walmart warns dressers could fall over
item.description: Walmart has issued a recall for a product that the United States Consumer Product Safety Commission says poses a "risk of serious injury or death.
item.dc:creator: Ben Mitchell, KRON4

item.title: Fox Exclusive: Nationwide human trafficking bust targets Chinese organized crime networks
item.description: Fox News joined Austin Police in a nationwide crackdown on illicit Chinese massage parlors tied to human trafficking, rescuing victims and arresting traffickers.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Dozens of costumed canines get their chance to trick-or-treating at Howloween event in Michigan
item.description: Dogs dressed up as everything from Elvis Presley to Scooby Doo’s Mystery Machine went trick-or-treating in Michigan.
item.dc:creator: Mike Householder, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump says he has commuted sentence of former US Rep. George Santos in federal fraud case
item.description: President Donald Trump said Friday he had commuted the sentence of former U.S. Rep. George Santos, who is serving more than seven years in federal prison after pleading guilty to fraud and identity theft charges.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: DA files for motion to resentence former youth minister convicted of sexual battery
item.description: The D.A. said that because she was found guilty, Mississippi law prohibits offenders from serving their sentence on house arrest.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Nguyen, NewsNation

item.title: Trio wearing Halloween costumes caught on camera terrorizing family while attempting break-in
item.description: A trio wearing Halloween masks was caught on camera terrorizing a family while attempting to break into their home.
item.dc:creator: Katlyn Fernandez, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Rhode Island teacher accused of sexually harassing former student
item.description: The student told investigators the teacher, who was his physical education teacher, began texting his personal cell phone after finding his number online.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Bawden, NewsNation

item.title: Kevin Federline says his sons with Britney Spears are the reason for his new memoir
item.description: Kevin Federline says concern for his two sons with Britney Spears long kept him from telling his story, and those same concerns are the reason he's telling it now that they're men.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DALTON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: LA county reaches tentative $828M agreement to settle 400 more claims of sexual abuse
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Los Angeles County has reached a tentative $828 million agreement to settle more than 400 additional sexual abuse claims against county employees, months after approving the largest sexual abuse settlement in U.S. history, officials said Friday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Indiana University fires student newspaper adviser who refused to block news stories
item.description: Tension between Indiana University and its student newspaper flared this week with the elimination of the outlet's print editions and the firing of a faculty adviser, who refused an order to keep news stories out of a homecoming edition.
item.dc:creator: TODD RICHMOND, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Arizona executes a man for killing 4 members of a family in Phoenix in 1993
item.description: A man convicted of killing four members of a Phoenix family over 30 years ago in an act of revenge over stolen goods was put to death Friday in Arizona’s second execution of the year.
item.dc:creator: JACQUES BILLEAUD and SEJAL GOVINDARAO, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: New York GOP shuts down state's Young Republicans over group chat
item.description: The New York Republican State Committee’s leaders unanimously voted to disband the Empire State’s Young Republicans chapter on Friday after screenshots from a Telegram chat exposed members using antisemitic and racist language.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: The other Alaska airlift: Volunteers save dogs from a flooded Alaska village, 1 tiny plane at a time
item.description: A nonprofit in rural Alaska is working to rescue dogs from Kipnuk, a village hit hard by Typhoon Halong.
item.dc:creator: Cedar Attanasio, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: What is the 'AI homeless man prank'? Police says it's dangerous
item.description: The prank uses artificial intelligence to create a picture or video of a "homeless man" entering a person's home, going through their fridge, or lying in their bed.
item.dc:creator: Kyle Mitchell, NewsNation

item.title: With no takers yet, White House meets with colleges still weighing an agreement with Trump
item.description: Five remaining universities have been weighing an offer from President Donald Trump to sign a “compact” for higher education.
item.dc:creator: Collin Binkley, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Father pleads guilty to murder of missing 7-month-old son in Southern California
item.description: The father of a 7-month-old boy missing in Southern California pleaded guilty to second-degree murder of his son after a monthslong investigation that has failed to locate the child's remains.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Meta adding AI chatbot safety features for teens
item.description: Meta, the parent company of Instagram and Facebook, plans to roll out new safety features for its artificial intelligence (AI) chatbot.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, WFLA, NBC

item.title: GOP senators worry about Trump, Hegseth shutdown moves
item.description: Republican senators have questions and concerns about how President Donald Trump is managing to pay more than 1 million military service members during the government shutdown and are seeking more information about what funds he is dipping into to achieve his political ends.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Car going 100 mph leads deputies to man wanted in Florida teen's killing
item.description: A traffic stop led to the arrest of a man wanted for the murder of a 15-year-old girl in Brevard County, according to the Volusia County Sheriff's Office.
item.dc:creator: Alaina Papazian, NewsNation

item.title: Homeland Security says Marine's father who was deported had faced domestic violence, assault charges
item.description: SAN DIEGO (AP) — The father of a Marine who was arrested by immigration authorities when visiting his pregnant daughter at Camp Pendleton has a criminal record that includes charges of domestic violence and aggravated assault with a deadly weapon, the Department of Homeland Security said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Susan Stamberg, first woman to host a national news program, dies at age 87
item.description: Susan Stamberg, a “founding mother” of National Public Radio and the first female broadcaster to host a national news program, has died. She was 87. Stamberg died Thursday, NPR reported. It did not provide a cause of death.
item.dc:creator: AUDREY McAVOY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Judge wants immigration agents in Chicago area to wear body cameras after clashes with public
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Troubled by clashes between agents and the public, a judge on Thursday said she will require federal immigration officers in the Chicago area to wear body cameras, and she also summoned a senior official to court next week to discuss an enforcement operation that has resulted in more
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINE FERNANDO, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: 9 people have now been arrested in connection with the mass shooting in Mississippi, FBI says
item.description: LELAND, Miss. (AP) — Nine people have now been arrested in connection with a mass shooting in the Mississippi Delta town of Leland that left six people dead, an FBI special agent said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIE BATES, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Legendary founding KISS guitarist Ace Frehley dies at 74
item.description: (KRON) -- Legendary founding KISS guitarist Ace Frehley has died at the age of 74. He died peacefully surrounded by family in Morristown, New Jersey, according to his agent. Earlier on Thursday TMZ reported he was on life support, and the prognosis was "not good.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4

item.title: Red Cross sends help from Nevada to Alaska to support flooding evacuees
item.description: A trained volunteer from the American Red Cross of Southern Nevada was sent to Anchorage, Alaska, to help more than 2,000 people displaced by severe flooding.
item.dc:creator: Stephanie Overton, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Capital makeover: Trump shows off plans for giant arch in DC
item.description: President Trump on Wednesday detailed plans for an arch to be built near the Lincoln Memorial at the entrance to Washington, D.C., the latest instance of Trump's efforts to give the capital a makeover.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Colleges are fighting to prove their return on investment
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — For a generation of young Americans, choosing where to go to college — or whether to go at all — has become a complex calculation of costs and benefits that often revolves around a single question: Is the degree worth its price? Public confidence in higher education has plummeted i
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: The world is on track to add 57 superhot days a year, but it could have been worse: Study
item.description: The world is on track to add nearly two months of dangerous superhot days each year by the end of the century.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: This family visit to a military base ended with ICE deporting a Marine's dad
item.description: SAN DIEGO (AP) — Parents of a U.S. Marine were detained by federal immigration officials and one of them was later deported after visiting family members at a California military base, a case that has drawn attention to how the government’s immigration crackdown is touching military families.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: The Latest: Social Security cost-of-living increase announcement delayed by government shutdown
item.description: The ongoing government shutdown is delaying the announcement of the annual Social Security cost-of-living adjustment for tens of millions of beneficiaries. Originally scheduled for Wednesday, the 2024 Social Security COLA announcement will now be Oct. 24.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Who is President Dallin H. Oaks? A look back at his life before and during his ministry
item.description: The now 18th leader of the Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints was born on August 12, 1932 in Provo, UT. The Church website says Oaks and his late wife, June Dixon Oaks, had six children.
item.dc:creator: Spencer Mahon, ABC 4
